summary: Africa's most populous country and a top oil producer, a federal democracy split between a Muslim north and Christian south, with a vibrant tech and entertainment sector held back by insecurity and corruption.

history.detail: Nigeria becomes a federation of three regions.
Igbo southeast secedes; a brutal war and famine kill over a million.
Biafra reintegrated; oil boom reshapes the economy.
Decades of military rule end with elected civilian government.
Islamist insurgency erupts in the northeast; tens of thousands killed.
Mass youth protests against police brutality shake the country.

finance.currency_role: Nigerian naira (NGN); repeatedly devalued amid FX shortages
leverage_tools: Largest economy and population in Africa
Dangote Refinery — one of the world's largest

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Africa's largest startup funding market; home to fintech unicorns

resources.oil_gas: Largest oil producer in Africa; major LNG exporter
resources.water: Abundant in the south; Niger and Benue river systems
resources.agriculture: Cassava, yams, cocoa; large but underproductive sector

follow_the_money.detail: Crude exports fund most government income but feed corruption and theft
Film and Afrobeats are global cultural exports and soft power
Lagos startups attract the bulk of African venture capital

theories_disputed.detail: Large-scale illegal crude tapping in the Niger Delta, sometimes with official complicity.
Boko Haram abducted 276 schoolgirls in 2014; many remain missing.

connections.detail: Major infrastructure lender and railway builder
Counter-terror cooperation against Boko Haram and ISWAP
Competing for the title of Africa's leading power
